target.title: fix(zalouser): split messages at line boundaries instead of mid-word
target.body:
## TL;DR

Long bot responses were split mid-word because the default chunk mode cut at exactly 2000 characters with no regard for word or sentence boundaries.

---

## Problem

`DEFAULT_TEXT_CHUNK_MODE = "length"` cuts the outgoing message at exactly the 2000-character limit regardless of whether that lands in the middle of a word. In practice this was observed as a Vietnamese word like "lên" arriving as two separate messages — "lê" followed by "n" — which is unreadable.

The `"newline"` chunk mode already exists in the codebase and correctly breaks at paragraph boundaries → single newlines → whitespace → hard cut (as a last resort). It was never set as the default, so callers that did not explicitly pass `textChunkMode: "newline"` got the hard-cut behavior.

---

## Invariant

> When a message is split into chunks, each chunk boundary must fall at a natural break (paragraph, newline, or whitespace) unless no such break exists within the limit window.

---

## What this PR does

- Changes `DEFAULT_TEXT_CHUNK_MODE` from `"length"` to `"newline"` in `send.ts`
- Adds one regression test: a message with a paragraph break inside the 2000-char window is split at the break, not at position 2000

---

## Why this matters

- **User impact**: Words arrived split across two Zalo messages, making bot responses unreadable in long replies.
- **System impact**: The hard-cut behavior never had a use case — `"newline"` falls back to a hard cut when no natural break exists, so it is strictly better for all inputs.

---

## Safety

- The `splitTextRangesByPreferredBreaks` function (used by `"newline"` mode) falls back to a hard cut when no natural break point is found, so behavior for texts with no whitespace is unchanged.
- All existing tests pass unchanged: they use continuous character strings with no natural break points, which fall through to the same hard-cut behavior as before.
- No change to any other logic.

---

## Test plan

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/zalouser/src/send.test.ts
```

- [x] All existing tests pass (13)
- [x] New test: paragraph break within window → split at break, not at position 2000

target.title: fix(zalouser): surface send errors and recover from oversized style payloads
target.body:
## TL;DR

Zalo delivery failures were completely invisible: the monitor never checked send results, error codes were lost, and messages silently disappeared when styled payloads exceeded Zalo's size limit.

---

## Problem

Three compounding gaps caused messages to vanish without any log trace:

1. `sendZaloTextMessage` caught errors but discarded the numeric `error_code` from Zalo's response — callers could not distinguish a transient network error from a Zalo-specific rejection.
2. The `sendText` and `sendMedia` callbacks in the monitor called `sendMessageZalouser` without checking the returned result, so `ok=false` responses were silently dropped.
3. Zalo rejects messages with `error_code=112` when the combined params payload (message text + `textProperties` JSON for styling) exceeds its server-side size limit. Empirically: a ~1420-char Vietnamese message combined with 56+ style ranges (~1871 chars of JSON) triggers this. The message was never delivered and no error appeared in logs.

---

## Invariant

> When Zalo rejects a styled message with error code 112, the message text must still be delivered (without styles), and the failure must be logged if delivery ultimately fails.

---

## What this PR does

- Adds `errorCode?: number` to `ZaloSendResult` so callers can branch on specific Zalo error codes
- Enhances `toErrorMessage` to include the numeric code in the string so it appears in logs
- Extracts the numeric error code from caught errors in `sendZaloTextMessage` and attaches it to the result
- Adds a retry-without-styles path in `sendMessageZalouser` on error code 112: if the styled send fails with 112, the message is re-sent as plain text
- Makes `sendText` and `sendMedia` in the monitor check `result.ok` and emit a structured error log when delivery fails

---

## Why this matters

- **User impact**: Long bot responses with markdown formatting silently failed to reach Zalo users. The bot appeared to respond on the web UI but nothing arrived in the chat.
- **System impact**: Failures were completely unobservable — no logs, no error propagation — making debugging impossible without adding instrumentation manually.

---

## Safety

- No change to message delivery path when everything succeeds
- The retry only triggers on the specific error code 112 with styled content; all other errors are forwarded unchanged
- Existing tests unchanged; 3 new regression tests added

---

## Test plan

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/zalouser/src/send.test.ts
```

- [x] All existing tests pass (13)
- [x] New test: retries without styles on error code 112
- [x] New test: propagates error when retry also fails
- [x] New test: does not retry when there are no styles

target.title: fix(sandbox): bump config hash when buildSandboxCreateArgs flags change
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a `SANDBOX_DOCKER_CREATE_ARGS_EPOCH` constant and a `createArgsEpoch` field to both `SandboxHashInput` and `SandboxBrowserHashInput`, so the existing config-hash recreate path also fires when `buildSandboxCreateArgs` adds, removes, or changes container creation flags.

**This PR adds the mechanism only.** It does not ship any flag change itself. Whichever PR adds the first real flag change (for example #74083 adding `--init`) bumps the epoch from the bootstrap value in the same release as the flag.

## Why

`buildSandboxCreateArgs` already drives container recreation via the labelled `openclaw.configHash`, but the hash inputs only capture mount layout (`mountFormatVersion`) and browser security flags (`securityEpoch`). Other docker create flags are invisible to the hash. So adding any new flag in a future PR (`--init`, cgroup limits, sysctls, capabilities, namespace flags, etc.) does NOT invalidate the hash, and existing containers keep running with their old flags forever.

The most-affected pattern is exactly the one #68691 reports: long-running sandbox containers (`scope: "agent"` + `prune.maxAgeDays: 0`) stay alive for weeks and never recreate naturally. Without an epoch in the hash, they never pick up new flags.

## What this PR adds

1. **`SANDBOX_DOCKER_CREATE_ARGS_EPOCH`** in `src/agents/sandbox/constants.ts`, set to a neutral bootstrap value (`"2026-05-bootstrap"`) and date-tagged so reviewers can tell at a glance which release introduced the bump.
2. **Header comment on the constant** explicitly telling future contributors to bump the epoch in the same PR (or a follow-up PR) as the actual flag change, never speculatively. Pre-bumping under a name like `"init-tini"` would let containers be recreated with a hash claiming the new behaviour while the actual create args still lack it.
3. **`createArgsEpoch: string`** in both `SandboxHashInput` and `SandboxBrowserHashInput`. Mirrors the existing `securityEpoch` / `mountFormatVersion` pattern.
4. **Both call sites updated** (`docker.ts:ensureSandboxContainer`, `browser.ts:ensureSandboxBrowser`) to pass the new constant.
5. **`openclaw.createArgsEpoch=...` container label** stamped at create time, alongside the existing `openclaw.mountFormatVersion` label, for diagnostics.
6. **Tests** (`config-hash.test.ts`, `docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts`) updated; new test "changes when docker create args epoch changes" mirrors the existing security-epoch test using neutral `"epoch-v1"`/`"epoch-v2"` sentinels.
7. **CHANGELOG** entry under `## Unreleased > ### Fixes` describing the mechanism, with `Thanks @aaajiao.` per repo convention.

## Reusability

When future PRs add or change container flags, the contributor only needs to bump the `SANDBOX_DOCKER_CREATE_ARGS_EPOCH` string in the same change and add a one-line history entry to the comment. No new types, no new test scaffolding, no risk of forgetting to invalidate the hash.

## Relationship to #74083 (and #68691)

Originally I had named this epoch after the `--init` rollout. Per clawsweeper P1 review, that was wrong — it would let this PR claim a feature ship that lives in a separate PR. The current value is neutral; the `--init` rollout in #74083 is responsible for bumping the epoch when it lands. With both merged in the same release, the long-running-container pattern reported in #68691 is fully addressed.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/config-hash.test.ts` — existing + new tests pass locally
- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts` — recreate path still asserts hash mismatch triggers `docker rm -f`
- [ ] Maintainer-side: full CI pass (note: the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-cli` failures in `plugins-location-bridges.test.ts` reproduce on upstream/main — see commit `5ecd01ff9` — and have no overlap with sandbox)
- [ ] Live verification: a future flag change (such as #74083) bumps the epoch and a long-running `scope: "agent"` sandbox is recreated on next agent run

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \

  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \

  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:23:00
   Duration  3.17s
```

**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

Simulated v2026.5.28 upgrade from scratch state dir with shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`:

```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-proof-upgrade openclaw doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
```

Doctor output:
```
[state-migrations] Auto-migrated legacy state:
- Migrated 1 Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) entry → plugin state
- Archived Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) legacy source →
  command-deploy-cache.json.migrated
```

SQLite verification after migration:
```
sqlite3 ... "SELECT plugin_id, namespace, entry_key, value_json
  FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"
→ discord|slash-command-deploy-hashes|111122223333:default|
  {"version":1,"hashes":{"global:reconcile":"aaa...a",
  "guild:1471499665584296039":"bbb...b"}}
```

Legacy file status:
```
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json       # → No such file (migrated)
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json.migrated  # → archived copy exists
```

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.
- Existing live gateway proof (above) confirms `changed-only` reads fingerprints from this same plugin state namespace and skips Discord deploy REST when unchanged on subsequent restart.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-account live gateway restart post-upgrade (simulated config with single default account only)
- `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
- Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit

target.title: fix(cli): clarify safe restart bounded-then-force behavior in help and docs
target.body:
## Summary
Clarify that `daemon restart --safe` uses a bounded deferred wait (default 5 minutes via `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs`) and may force a restart after the timeout expires. Update CLI help text, user-facing status messages, and daemon docs to reflect the actual bounded-then-force behavior.

Fixes #92194

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: The `--safe` restart CLI help and docs described "restart after active work drains" without mentioning the bounded default wait or that the restart can be forced after the `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` budget expires. Users relied on `--safe` semantics and were surprised when active work was cut.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 22.19, Linux
- **Code analysis path**: See root cause analysis (5 Whys) in commit
- **Test framework**: Vitest

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
cd /home/0668001085/workspace/openclaw-worktrees/fix/issue-92194
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/restart.test.ts
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/daemon.md
npx oxlint --tsconfig=tsconfig.json src/cli/daemon-cli/register-service-commands.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
✓ 3 test files passed (39 tests)
✓ Docs MDX check passed
✓ oxlint: no issues
```

**Observed result after the fix**: CLI `--help` output, deferred restart status messages, and daemon docs now accurately describe the bounded-then-force default behavior of `--safe` restart and point operators to `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs=0` for indefinite wait.

**What was not tested**: Live `openclaw daemon restart --safe` CLI command execution (docs/help-text-only change, no runtime behavior modified).

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 39/39 passed (lifecycle 25, restart.deferral-timeout 7, restart 7) |
| Type Check | ✅ | No type errors in changed files |
| Lint | ✅ | oxlint clean, oxfmt compliant |
| Docs Check | ✅ | check-docs-mdx passed |

## Risk checklist
**Did user-visible behavior change?** `No` - Only CLI help text, status messages, and documentation wording changed. Runtime restart behavior is unchanged.
**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No`
**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No`

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested

target.title: fix(memory-core): add actionable remediation hints for memory search embedding errors
target.body:
## Summary

Fresh port of #55684 onto current `main` (the source branch was 16k commits behind and unmergeable). Adds actionable remediation hints when `memory_search` or `openclaw memory status` surfaces an embedding-provider failure, so leaked-key, quota, and invalid-auth errors stop printing as raw provider strings without recovery guidance.

Fixes #54912. Supersedes #55684 (closed-stale).

## What changed

- **`extensions/memory-core/src/tools.shared.ts`** — adds `resolveEmbeddingErrorKind` (classifies leaked / quota / invalid_key) and `resolveEmbeddingErrorHint`. `buildMemorySearchUnavailableResult` now prefers the kind-specific hint over the previous generic `action` text.
- **`extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts`** — adds `resolveEmbeddingErrorRemediation` with provider-specific env-var hints (`GEMINI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`) and emits a `Fix` line in `runMemoryStatus` after `Embeddings error`.
- **Tests** — `tools.test.ts` and `tools.citations.test.ts` updated for the new quota action text; new tests for leaked-key and invalid-key paths.
- **`CHANGELOG.md`** — single-line entry under Unreleased › Fixes (per repo policy and Codex P3 review on #55684).

## Codex review notes addressed (from #55684)

- ✅ Branch is now on current `main` (no longer 16k commits diverged).
- ✅ CHANGELOG entry added (the P3 finding from the prior Codex pass).
- ✅ Focused tests for leaked-key, quota, and invalid-key classification added in `tools.test.ts`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 …` — clean.
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts` — 20/20 pass (2 new tests included).
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts` — 48/48 pass.
- [x] `pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main` reports only `extension production` + `extension test`; CHANGELOG is doc-only.
- [x] Verified the `pnpm tsgo:extensions` errors in `src/secrets/runtime-web-tools*` and `extensions/google/web-search-provider.test.ts` are pre-existing on `upstream/main` (unrelated to this change).

target.title: fix(memory): improve QMD recall for channel queries
target.body:
## Summary

- allow QMD recall for Discord channel sessions in the default scope
- retry empty/noisy QMD search queries once with a tighter lexical query built from priority memory anchors
- report scope-denied QMD searches in debug output instead of making them indistinguishable from true zero-hit searches

## Why

Gary's live Samantha tests in Discord showed `memory_search` using QMD but returning zero hits for a noisy memory query. Local log inspection showed the first failure mode was QMD scope denial for Discord channel sessions. After allowing channel sessions, the intentionally noisy query could reach QMD, but needed a conservative lexical retry to recover useful memory hits rather than stopping at an empty result.

This stays separate from #75761, which handles active-memory timeout behavior.

## Validation

- `pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-scope.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- live Samantha Discord test: the noisy query `thread memory retrieval active-memory QMD semantic memory search Discord test` returned 4 QMD hits with `fallback: relaxed-lexical-zero-hit`, and Samantha was able to follow with `memory_get`

target.title: fix(clawdock): open dashboard via clawdock-dashboard on published port instead of hardcoded default port
target.body:
## Summary

  - Problem: `clawdock-dashboard` could open the container-internal dashboard URL/port instead of the Docker-published host port.
  - Why it matters: Docker users with remapped ports could get a dashboard URL that does not work from the host browser, even though the gateway is healthy.
  - What changed: The helper now maps loopback dashboard URLs to the published Docker Compose gateway port, preserves scheme/path, appends the resolved auth token for browser launch, redacts the token in terminal output, and avoids fragile external command dependencies on the dashboard path.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No gateway runtime, dashboard UI, Docker Compose service, plugin, or API behavior changed.
  - Fixes #77344

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/77344
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

  - Root cause: `openclaw dashboard --no-open` runs inside the Docker Compose network namespace and reports the gateway’s internal loopback URL/port. `clawdock-dashboard` reused that URL directly instead of translating it to the host-published Compose port.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: No ClawDock-specific smoke test covered Docker host-port remapping, token redaction, or fresh zsh shell compatibility for the shell helper.
  - Contributing context (if known): The issue appears when `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT` differs from the internal gateway port `18789`.


  ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - Coverage level that should have caught this:
    - [ ] Unit test
    - [x] Seam / integration test
    - [ ] End-to-end test
    - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: A future ClawDock helper shell smoke test for `clawdock-dashboard`.
  - Scenario the test should lock in: Given Docker Compose publishes `openclaw-gateway` on a host port, `clawdock-dashboard` opens that
  published host URL, preserves scheme/path, includes the auth token only in the browser URL, and redacts it from terminal output.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior depends on shell helper parsing plus Docker Compose port output, not gateway
  internals.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): None found for `scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh`.
  - If no new test is added, why not: This PR keeps the change scoped to the existing standalone shell helper, which does not currently have a
  test harness.

  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  `clawdock-dashboard` now opens the host-accessible Docker-published dashboard URL, such as `http://127.0.0.1:3200/`, instead of the
  container-internal `18789` URL. Tokenized URLs are still used for browser auth, but terminal output shows `#token=<redacted>`.

  ## Diagram (if applicable)

  ```text
  Before:
  clawdock-dashboard -> openclaw-cli inside Docker -> http://127.0.0.1:18789/ -> host browser may fail

  After:
  clawdock-dashboard -> openclaw-cli inside Docker -> remap to Compose published port -> host browser opens dashboard
  ```

  ## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (No)
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (Yes)
  - New/changed network calls? (No)
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
  - Data access scope changed? (No)
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: The helper now reconstructs the browser URL with an auth token when the CLI indicates token auto-
    auth is available. The token is sent only to the browser/open command and is redacted from terminal output and fallback instructions.

  ## Repro + Verification

  ### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: Docker Compose, openclaw-gateway published on host port 3200
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): ClawDock dashboard helper
  - Relevant config (redacted): OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<redacted>, gateway internal port 18789, published host port 3200

  ### Steps

  1. Start the OpenClaw Docker Compose gateway with a host-published gateway port different from 18789.
  2. Source scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh.
  3. Run clawdock-dashboard.

  ### Expected

  - The helper opens the host-published dashboard URL.
  - The browser/open command receives the tokenized URL.
  - Terminal output redacts the token.

  ### Actual

  - Before: the helper used the CLI-reported internal dashboard URL.
  - After: the helper opens http://127.0.0.1:3200/#token=<redacted> in the local Docker smoke test.

  ## Evidence

  - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
  - [x] Trace/log snippets
  - [ ] Screenshot/recording
  - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

  Local verification included:

  ```text
  ✅ Opening: http://127.0.0.1:3200/#token=<redacted>

  Also run:

  bash -n scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh
  zsh -n scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh
  pnpm build
  pnpm check

  pnpm test was attempted but hit unrelated failures in agent/provider/plugin shards before completion.

  ```

  ## Human Verification (required)

  - Verified scenarios: Shell syntax under bash and zsh; fresh zsh invocation; Docker Compose gateway port resolution; `clawdock-dashboard` opens the published host port with browser launch stubbed; token is redacted in displayed output.
  - Edge cases checked: IPv4 wildcard publish address, IPv6 wildcard publish address, URL encoding for tokens, preserving scheme/path during
  port remap, config-token precedence over project `.env` token, avoiding zsh `path` variable shadowing, avoiding `head`/`tr`/`grep` dependencies on the dashboard path.
  - What you did **not** verify: Full clean `pnpm test`; it was attempted but failed in unrelated shards before completion.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

  - Risk: Token handling in the shell helper could expose the dashboard token in terminal logs.
    - Mitigation: The browser/open command receives the tokenized URL, but displayed and fallback output use `#token=<redacted>`.

  - Risk: Remapping could break non-root dashboard paths or TLS.
    - Mitigation: The helper preserves the original URL scheme and path while replacing only the host/port authority.

  - Risk: Shell helper behavior may vary between bash and zsh or minimal PATH environments.
    - Mitigation: The dashboard path avoids `head`, `tr`, and `grep`, avoids zsh’s special `path` variable, and was smoke-tested from a fresh zsh invocation.

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

  - Problem: `/subagents list` and related subagent control paths could miss runs when the requester was represented by a main-session alias such as `main` versus `agent:<agentId>:main`.
  - Why it matters: Users could spawn subagents successfully but fail to list or control them from the main session, making background work appear lost.
  - What changed: Canonicalized main-session requester/controller keys at the subagent list/ control boundaries and added regression coverage for both tool and slash-command paths.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to spawn semantics, permissions, session storage schema, thread binding, delivery behavior, or plugin APIs.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Auth / tokens

- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

- Closes #75593
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

## Evidence

## Human Verification (required)

target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.


## Live exact-head capture (2026-06-12): found and fixed an emit-side gap

Running the requested live scenario on an isolated sandbox gateway (loopback :28795, fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, Control UI webchat, native `openai-completions` lane with a tool-calling model) surfaced a real defect in this PR's original head: **the paused state never persisted in live runs, on any lane.** A store probe polling every 0.3s across four real `sessions_spawn` + `sessions_yield` turns at head `be96a422ff` captured the persisted row going `running -> killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` at yield time -- the exact pre-PR failure this PR exists to prevent.

**Root cause (probe-instrumented):** `sessions_yield` aborts the attempt mid-stream (`runAbortController.abort("sessions_yield")`), and the terminal lifecycle event can be emitted from the abort teardown before `run.ts` post-processing writes the `yielded` meta. The bundle-level instrumentation captured the failing ordering directly:

```
emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = undefined  aborted = false   <- event emitted first
meta.yielded set -> false                                          <- run.ts wrote 551ms later
```

The event reaches `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` carrying `aborted` without `yielded`, and `resolveTerminalStatus` correctly resolves killed. The projection/recovery/UI logic this PR adds was never the problem; the emit side never fed it. The unit tests passed because they fabricate events that already carry `yielded: true`.

**Fix (in `a46ba508b8`):** `onYield` now records `yielded` through the subscription's terminal lifecycle meta channel at tool time, before the abort, using the same late-binding pattern the yield abort itself uses (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). A regression test pins the handler half: an end event with `state.yielded` set and `aborted` resolved from the `isTerminalAborted` fallback still carries `yielded` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`).

**Post-fix live proof, end to end (same sandbox, instrumented bundle):**

```
10:27:40.065  [PROBE] meta.yielded set -> true              (onYield records at tool time)
10:27:40.423  [PROBE] emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = true   (event carries it)
10:27:4x      store: status=paused pauseReason=sessions_yield abortedLastRun=false
~10:28        Sessions Control UI: main session "Paused", subagent "Live"
10:28:1x      gateway KILLED while paused -> store unchanged (paused, ab=false)
10:28:3x      gateway restarted -> recovery skips the paused session;
              no "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted" prompt in the chat
10:29:20      subagent (orphan-recovered post-restart) completes -> announce drains
              the queued continuation in order -> running, pauseReason cleared
10:30:48      continuation finishes -> done, abortedLastRun=false
```

Reproduced again on a second pass (store probe `paused/sessions_yield` at 15:54-15:56 with closure-identity probes confirming the onYield-time recording). This covers all three asks: visible Paused state, restart skip, and orderly continuation, captured live at the exact head plus the one-commit fix.

**Codex lane now covered too (was a sibling-lane gap, now fixed in this PR).** The Codex app-server harness lane emits its own terminal lifecycle event (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` `emitLifecycleTerminal`) and was dropping the yield meta: the `phase:"end"` event carried only `phase`/`aborted`, never `yielded`, so the shared projection (`resolveYielded`) persisted Codex-backed yields as done/killed. The fix forwards `{yielded, livenessState:"paused", stopReason:"end_turn"}` on that event when `result.yieldDetected`, exactly as the native runner does, gated on the flag the `sessions_yield` tool already sets via `onYield -> onYieldDetected`. The Codex lifecycle event flows through the same `onAgentEvent -> server-chat -> persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent -> resolveYielded` path as the native lane.

Proven live on the Codex lane: a real `openai/gpt-5.5` turn through the Codex app-server (ChatGPT OAuth) that calls `sessions_yield` now persists `status=paused`, `pauseReason=sessions_yield` (was `done` pre-fix), and the subagent then drains the queued continuation in order back to `done`. An integration test (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.yield-lifecycle.test.ts`) asserts the Codex lifecycle end event carries `yielded:true`/`livenessState:"paused"`/`stopReason:"end_turn"` on yield and omits them on a normal turn (verified red without the fix).

Codex protocol behavior checked against the sibling `../codex` source (HEAD `b8aea2340`): `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/schema/typescript/ServerRequest.ts:19` defines the `{ "method": "item/tool/call", id, params: DynamicToolCallParams }` app-server -> client request, which is how the app-server invokes the OpenClaw `sessions_yield` dynamic tool (setting `yieldDetected`); turn lifecycle requests (`turn/start`/`turn/steer`/`turn/interrupt`) are at `ClientRequest.ts:90`. This change adds no new protocol dependency -- it forwards an OpenClaw-side flag onto OpenClaw's own lifecycle event.

target.title: fix(gateway): make `gateway start` idempotent when already running
target.body:
Fixes #60087.

## Summary

If you have `openclaw gateway start` in your `~/.profile` (the setup hint adds it), every exec tool call ends up SIGTERM'ing the running gateway and dropping your WebSocket session.

The chain:

```
exec tool → bash -l -c "env -0" (PATH probe)
         → ~/.profile sources → openclaw gateway start
         → systemctl restart → SIGTERM
```

`gateway start` was unconditionally calling the platform restart action. It now early-returns when the service is already running.

`start` means "ensure running". Use `openclaw gateway restart` if you want to force a cycle.

## Changes

- New `"already-running"` outcome on `GatewayServiceStartResult`.
- `startGatewayService` skips `service.restart()` when `state.running` is true.
- CLI emits a friendly "service is already running" message instead of pretending it restarted.
- Fixed two existing lifecycle tests that were named like "stopped service" / "scheduled restart" scenarios but never overrode the default `running` mock — they passed only because the old code path was unconditional.

Credit to @Slybry2000 for the report and trace.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/daemon/service.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` — all pass
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:test:src`, `oxfmt --check` clean
- [ ] `pnpm check:changed` in Testbox before merge

## Related (not duplicates)

This is a distinct mechanism from the prior cluster of "exec triggers gateway SIGTERM" fixes. Each addressed a different layer:

- #62086 (closed) — Docker `OPENCLAW_SHELL=exec` runtime-wrapper SIGTERM path (the second mechanism @amnesia106 reported in the issue thread).
- #32348 (closed) — Linux/systemd restart-loop angle when restart was issued from inside an agent session.
- #41198, #41752, #41815, #41978 (all closed) — macOS LaunchAgent restart semantics (kickstart/bootout/auto-restart).
- #30083, #40799, #11187 (all closed) — earlier exec-triggered SIGTERM paths now resolved.

This PR closes the remaining gap: making `startGatewayService()` itself idempotent so the `~/.profile` re-invocation doesn't trigger a platform restart in the first place. Complementary to the above, not duplicative.

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60087

target.title: feat(ui): replace textarea with CodeMirror 6 JSON editor in raw config view
target.body:
## Summary

Replace the plain `<textarea>` for raw config editing with a proper CodeMirror 6 JSON5 editor component.

### Changes

**New file: `ui/src/ui/views/config-editor.ts`**
- CodeMirror 6 editor with JSON5 language mode (handles comments, unquoted keys, trailing commas)
- Syntax highlighting, fold gutter, line numbers, active line
- Real-time JSON5 linting with error markers (red dot in gutter)
- Format/prettify button (JSON5-aware via `JSON5.parse`)
- Read-only mode via `Compartment` (live toggle without recreating editor)
- Dark/light theme via `themeCompartment`
- Full dark theme: `#1f2937` background, `#e5e7eb` text
- `errorOffset()` handles both JSON5 ("at line:col") and JSON ("position N") error formats

**`ui/src/ui/views/config.ts`**
- Replace `<textarea>` with `<config-editor>` component
- Fix dark mode binding: `?dark` now correctly follows `system` mode via `prefersLightScheme()`

**`ui/src/ui/views/config.browser.test.ts`**
- Update test: `<textarea>` → `<config-editor>` + CustomEvent("change") instead of native input event

**`ui/package.json`** — new dependencies
- `codemirror-json5`, `json5`, `lezer-json5`
- `@codemirror/{autocomplete,commands,lang-json,language,lint,state,view}`

**`CHANGELOG.md`**
- One-liner entry under `### Changes`

### Motivation

OpenClaw raw config uses JSON5 format (comments, trailing commas). Plain `<textarea>` can't validate or highlight it. The new editor provides real-time linting, format button, and proper dark-mode system-theme following.

### Test plan

- [x] ESLint / TypeScript checks pass
- [x] Config editor renders in light and dark modes
- [x] System dark mode correctly triggers dark theme
- [x] JSON5 linting marks errors with red gutter dots
- [x] Format button prettifies valid JSON5 content
- [x] Read-only mode disables editing
- [x] Undo/redo (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y) works
- [x] Browser test updated and passes

Closes #56401

target.title: Gateway: guard session-run cancel requester in minimal tests
target.body:
## Summary
- Rebased this replacement lane onto latest `origin/main` and kept the minimal-test-gateway requester guard from the prior patch.
- Bounded sticky session-run cancel replay state with a 10 minute TTL plus a 1,000-entry cap, while preserving late-handler replay semantics.
- Added a production cleanup path: registered chat/agent run teardown now clears the matching sticky cancel target after the owning run finishes/aborts.
- Documented `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime` in `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`, including supported imports (`onSessionRunCancel`, `requestSessionRunCancel`, public types) and the explicit note that the core-owned emitter is not public.
- Added the required `CHANGELOG.md` Unreleased entry for the Plugin SDK import path and Gateway cancel behavior.

## Integration note
- #75371 remains the clean replacement lane for #68624. It no longer carries the earlier stacked `sessions_send` scope from #68622.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Plugin-owned delegated tasks can use `openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime` to request cancellation of the owning Gateway session run; Gateway aborts the run, emits the core-owned cancel to plugin observers, preserves sticky replay briefly for late registration, and clears sticky replay during run cleanup. The core-owned emitter remains unavailable through the public plugin SDK subpath.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `f9343ffcda` on Node `v22.22.2` in the repository worktree, using the real in-process Gateway chat-abort runtime (`registerChatAbortController` + `wireSessionRunCancelRequester`) and the plugin-facing session-run-cancel runtime import path. No external network sends or secrets were used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a one-off local runtime proof script with `node --import tsx <proof-script>.mts`. The script registered a real Gateway chat abort controller, wired `wireSessionRunCancelRequester(...)`, imported `onSessionRunCancel` and `requestSessionRunCancel` through the plugin SDK runtime source path, requested cancel for `{ kind:"session_run", sessionKey:"agent:proof:main", runId:"proof-run-1" }`, then checked the Gateway abort signal, plugin observer callback, sticky replay before cleanup, and cleanup state after `active.cleanup()`.

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the local proof command:

```json
{
  "proof": "local gateway chat-abort runtime + public plugin SDK session-run-cancel-runtime",
  "publicPluginImport": "openclaw/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime",
  "coreEmitterPublic": false,
  "requestResult": { "requested": true, "aborted": true },
  "abortSignalAborted": true,
  "pluginObservedCancel": {
    "target": { "kind": "session_run", "sessionKey": "agent:proof:main", "runId": "proof-run-1" },
    "reason": { "source": "chat-abort", "message": "real runtime proof" }
  },
  "stickyReplayBeforeRunCleanup": { "source": "chat-abort", "message": "real runtime proof" },
  "stickyReplayAfterRunCleanupCount": 0,
  "terminalStateAfterRunCleanup": false,
  "abortedBroadcast": { "runId": "proof-run-1", "sessionKey": "agent:proof:main", "seq": 5, "state": "aborted", "stopReason": "real runtime proof" },
  "nodeSessionEventCount": 1,
  "boundedTerminalMaxEntries": 1000,
  "boundedTerminalTtlMs": 600000
}
```

Observed result after fix: The plugin-side cancel request returned `{ requested:true, aborted:true }`; the Gateway AbortController signal was aborted; the plugin observer saw exactly one `{ source:"chat-abort" }` cancellation; late registration replayed the sticky cancel before run cleanup; run cleanup removed sticky replay (`stickyReplayAfterRunCleanupCount:0`, `terminalStateAfterRunCleanup:false`); and the Gateway emitted an aborted chat payload with the requested stop reason.

What was not tested: External channel delivery, remote production Gateway deployment, and third-party plugin package publishing were not exercised in this local proof.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/sessions/session-run-cancel.test.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-run-cancel-runtime.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-early.test.ts` → pass (6 files, 52 tests across configured projects)
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts --testNamePattern "smoke: supports abort and idempotent completion"` → pass
- `pnpm run plugin-sdk:api:gen && pnpm run plugin-sdk:api:check` → pass (`OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`)
- `pnpm run lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported` → pass
- `pnpm run format:docs:check` → pass
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` → pass
- `pnpm run lint:core` → pass
- `pnpm run build` → pass

target.title: fix(ui): improve Thai locale copy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the new Thai Control UI locale still had high-visible English fallback copy and a few unnatural Thai labels around common controls, the agent detail surface, files, channels, cron panels, and the language picker.
- Why it matters: Thai users see these strings immediately after selecting Thai, and the docs/UI translation pipeline needs one shared terminology style.
- What changed: added Thai UI/docs glossary terms, updated Thai translation memory, regenerated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts`, added a Thai copy regression test, and fixed the i18n generator process decoder so formatted multibyte locale output does not get replacement characters when stdout chunks split UTF-8 bytes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no locale registry changes, no supported-locale changes, no docs generated-output edits under `docs/th/**`, no Mintlify/navigation behavior changes, and no public API or runtime contract changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #70510
- Related #3460
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Thai was recently enabled in the Control UI picker, but the Thai translation memory still carried English fallbacks and weaker terminology for high-visible agent/file/channel labels. While regenerating, the formatter subprocess output path could also decode UTF-8 chunks with `String(chunk)`, which can corrupt Thai text if a multibyte character is split across chunks.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing i18n tests checked structural alignment and non-English fallback health, but did not lock specific Thai high-visible labels or replacement-character regressions.
- Contributing context (if known): #70510 tracks broader Thai docs feedback, so this PR intentionally updates glossary sources and Control UI copy without editing generated docs output.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Thai common, language picker, agent, file preview, and cron delivery copy stays localized and does not contain the prior replacement-character corruption.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these keys are generated output consumed directly by the Control UI, so a locale-level unit assertion catches both TM drift and generator corruption.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): structural locale alignment already existed in the same file, but it did not assert Thai wording quality.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Thai Control UI copy is more natural for high-visible labels such as Agent, Workspace, Tools, Channel, Default, Preview, language names, and file preview controls. Future Thai docs/UI translation runs also get the updated mixed technical glossary.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [mixed English fallbacks and awkward labels]

After:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [Thai localized labels using shared glossary terms]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4.1
- Runtime/container: Node v24.13.0, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI i18n
- Relevant config (redacted): no translation provider configured; existing TM cache used

### Steps

1. Regenerate Thai Control UI locale from source translation memory.
2. Run i18n drift, targeted locale tests, docs glossary checks, formatter checks, and changed gate.
3. Inspect the generated Thai bundle for replacement-character corruption.

### Expected

- Thai locale bundle stays generator-clean, high-visible Thai labels are localized, and glossary source files stay valid.

### Actual

- Expected behavior confirmed locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation run:

```text
node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts sync --locale th --write
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm test ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts
pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts
git diff --check
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm check:changed
```

Notes:

- `pnpm test ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts` passed 2 Vitest shards: 81 UI files / 786 UI tests and 1 unit-fast file / 3 tests.
- `pnpm check:changed` selected core, coreTests, docs, and tooling lanes and completed successfully.
- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because the local `blacksmith` CLI is not installed. The unpinned remote installer was not executed; the local changed gate above was used as the fallback.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: regenerated Thai locale from TM, verified `ui:i18n:check` clean for all locales, verified Thai high-visible copy assertions, verified docs Thai glossary source check, verified no replacement characters remain in the Thai locale/TM, and verified local changed gate.
- Edge cases checked: UTF-8 multibyte formatter output from the i18n generator, language picker Thai/English labels, agent workspace/file preview labels, and cron delivery summary label.
- What you did **not** verify: browser screenshot of the Control UI and Blacksmith Testbox, because the Blacksmith CLI was unavailable locally.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Thai wording preferences can be subjective.
  - Mitigation: kept the scope to high-visible UI labels and encoded the mixed technical style in both UI and docs glossaries so future translation passes stay consistent.
- Risk: generator script change touches shared tooling.
  - Mitigation: narrowed the change to UTF-8 stream decoding for subprocess output and covered the observed Thai replacement-character failure with a locale test plus `ui:i18n:check`.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Thai Control UI high-visible labels now load from the regenerated Thai locale bundle with localized agent, workspace, channel, file-preview, and language picker copy instead of the prior English/awkward fallback wording.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS 26.4.1 with Node v24.13.0 and pnpm 10.33.2, using the generated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts` bundle after the patch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Loaded the generated Thai Control UI locale bundle with Node/tsx and printed the labels a Thai user would see in the agent/file/language picker surfaces.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from a live `node` locale import in this OpenClaw checkout:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import { th } from \"./ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts\"; const proof = { commonDefault: th.agents.default, agentTabsTools: th.agents.tabs.tools, agentChannelsTitle: th.agents.channels.title, agentWorkspace: th.agents.context.workspace, filePreview: th.agents.files.preview, fileEmptyDraft: th.agents.files.emptyDraft, thaiLanguageLabel: th.languages.th }; console.log(JSON.stringify(proof, null, 2));"
{
  "commonDefault": "ค่าเริ่มต้น",
  "agentTabsTools": "เครื่องมือ",
  "agentChannelsTitle": "ช่องทาง",
  "agentWorkspace": "เวิร์กสเปซ",
  "filePreview": "ดูตัวอย่าง",
  "fileEmptyDraft": "ร่างว่าง",
  "thaiLanguageLabel": "ไทย"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The generated Thai bundle returns localized Thai strings for the visible Agent/Tools/Channel/Workspace/File Preview/Thai language labels, with no replacement-character corruption in the sampled output.
- **What was not tested**: Browser screenshot of the Control UI and Blacksmith Testbox, because the local `blacksmith` and `crabbox` CLIs are not installed in this environment.
